//: redb.Identity — UNIFIED external context.json (Tsak Layer 3).
PER-CONTEXT format (introduced together with TsakCoordinator support for
'Contexts:' root section): every key is scoped to a specific context (or the
shared 'default' bucket). Mirrors the host-appsettings shape exactly:
Tsak:Contexts:default     ↔ Contexts.default
Tsak:Contexts:{ctxName}   ↔ Contexts.{ctxName}
Devops single source of truth for both .tpkg modules:
- redb.Identity.Core.Module.tpkg (context 'identity.core')
- redb.Identity.Http.tpkg        (context 'identity.http')
DEPLOY: copy this file into the same Libs/ folder as the .tpkg files.
TsakCoordinator.LoadModuleConfigFiles parses {Libs}/context.json once per module:
1. merge Contexts.default (shared by every context),
2. merge Contexts.{moduleContextName} on top (context-specific overrides win).
Sections for OTHER contexts are simply ignored when loading a given module.
LEGACY-FALLBACK: if 'Contexts:' is absent, all root keys are merged flat for
every module (old behaviour) — keeps existing context.json files working.
5-LAYER PIPELINE:
L1 host appsettings:  Tsak:Contexts:default:*
L2 host appsettings:  Tsak:Contexts:{ctxName}:*
L3 THIS file:         Contexts.default + Contexts.{ctxName}
L4 in-tpkg stub:      ContextName + AutoStart only (module identity)
L5 Override (wins):   Tsak:Contexts:{ctxName}:Override:*
Environment-variable overrides (.NET IConfiguration mapping):
Tsak__Contexts__identity.core__Override__Identity__AccessTokenLifetime=01:00:00
Tsak__Contexts__identity.core__Override__Identity__RateLimit__Enabled=true
Tsak__Contexts__identity.http__Override__IdentityTransport__Http__PublicPort=5002
SECRETS — never commit real secrets to this file. Supply via L5 Override:
Identity:RecoveryCodePepper                              (base64 random)
Identity:SeedBackchannelClient:ClientSecret              (base64 random; SAME value also set as Identity__BackchannelClient__ClientSecret on the Web BFF)
Identity:DynamicRegistrationInitialAccessToken           (bearer token)
Identity:FederationProviders[i]:ClientSecret             (per provider)
Identity:Ldap:Providers[i]:BindPassword                  (per provider)
Identity:RateLimit:RedisConnectionString                 (if Redis backend)
Identity:FederationState:RedisConnectionString           (if Redis backend)
Redb:{instance}:ConnectionString                         (DB credentials)
LOGGING — log-level overrides DO NOT belong in this file. Serilog is wired in
redb.Tsak.Worker/Program.cs via UseSerilog(.ReadFrom.Configuration(...)) and reads
from the ROOT 'Serilog:*' section of the host configuration. TsakCoordinator merges
context.json only into the per-context bucket 'Tsak:Contexts:{ctxName}:*' (which
then binds the module Options classes), so anything written here under a 'Serilog'
key — or as an L5 Override env-var like Tsak__Contexts__identity.core__Override__Serilog__... —
lands in a namespace Serilog doesn't read. Identity-stack noise (OpenIddict per-
request INF, etc.) is silenced in:
redb.Tsak/src/redb.Tsak.Worker/appsettings.json -> Serilog:MinimumLevel:Override
Or as an env-var directly on the root key:
Serilog__MinimumLevel__Override__OpenIddict=Warning

//default: Shared bucket: keys here are merged into EVERY context loaded from this folder.
Use only for things that genuinely need to be the same across contexts:
• Redb named instances (DataProtection key-ring lives in this DB — must match).
• RedbInstanceName  (HTTP facade reads it as a top-level property).

identity-sqlite.//: SQLite mirror — toggle 'RedbInstanceName' below to switch the whole Identity stack to SQLite. EnsureCreated bootstraps the schema on first run. Pro tier so PropsSaveStrategy=ChangeTracking works.

default.//RedbInstanceName: Switch this between 'identity-pg' and 'identity-sqlite' to flip the entire Identity stack between providers. The Identity:RedbInstanceName under identity.core defaults to the same name.

//identity.core: Identity:* keys live here. The HTTP facade (identity.http) ignores this section.

Identity.//RedbInstanceName: Must match Contexts.default.RedbInstanceName above. Flip between 'identity-pg' and 'identity-sqlite' (or any other instance you register in Contexts.default.Redb) to switch the whole Identity stack between providers.
Identity.//IssuerNote: MUST match redb.Identity.Web appsettings.json -> Identity:Authority / Identity:ApiBaseUrl and the Issuer claim emitted in id_token. PROD: front with a TLS-terminating reverse proxy and set Issuer to the public https URL.
Identity.//RequirePkce: Mandatory PKCE (OAuth 2.1) is the in-code default (true). Set false to ALSO accept plain authorization_code without a code_challenge — required to certify the OpenID 'Basic OP' profile, whose non-PKCE tests expect the OP to accept such requests. PKCE, when sent, is still S256-only. PROD: leave true (or omit).
//UsePropsSigningKeyStore: When true, signing keys are persisted in the props store (DataProtection-encrypted)
and the JWKS endpoint serves them live from the store on every request —
admin /signing-keys/rotate + /retire reflect in the JWKS immediately without
process restart. Required for the demo_jwks_rotation probe.
Caveat: OpenIddict still picks the signing credential for NEW tokens from its
frozen options.SigningCredentials list, populated at startup. After admin rotate,
new tokens continue using the OLD kid until the OpenIddict cache refreshes
(process restart, or a future IOptionsMonitor wiring — separate batch).
Dev relaxes DataProtection.RequireAtRestEncryption because no MasterKey is
configured locally — production deployments MUST configure one of:
//DynamicRegistrationThrottle: DCR rate-limit (per remote IP). The in-code default of 5 per 10 s now ships with
RFC 6585 semantics (the throttle was switched to .RejectOnOverflow() — overflow
returns 429 + Retry-After instead of silently semaphore-waiting). Dev still bumps
the budget so the run_all suite, which DCRs many clients back-to-back from the
same loopback IP, doesn't trip the rate limit during normal development. A
Production should KEEP the in-code default 5/10 s (or tighter) for security.
//Features: Single source of truth for module-level on/off toggles. Both Core (DI / direct-vm)
and Http (HTTP endpoint mounting) read this same section — one declaration
drives both modules. Toggle here, never in IdentityTransport:Features below.
//DynamicRegistrationAllowedScopesSecuri: Dev defaults — widened to include every granular identity:<resource>:<action>
scope so the included demo suite can probe each admin surface independently.
THIS IS PERMISSIVE: any caller of POST /connect/register can mint a
Production hosts MUST either (a) set DynamicRegistrationInitialAccessToken so DCR
requires an out-of-band bearer, or (b) trim this list back to user-info scopes
(openid, profile, email, phone, address, offline_access, groups, roles, identity:account).
those carry the highest blast radius (full admin / act-as-another-user).
Identity.//DynamicRegistrationInitialAccessToken: SECRET. Generate: [Convert]::ToBase64String((1..32 | %{Get-Random -Max 256})). Supply via L5 Override env-var.
//WebAuthn: MFA-3 (FIDO2 / Passkeys). When Enabled=true the self-service /me/webauthn route
and the MFA assert route are exposed. RpId and Origins are CRYPTO-BOUND to every
registered credential — changing them after registrations breaks all keys.
Dev defaults below match the BFF on https://localhost:7000.

//: Variant B integration — opt-in. When Enabled=true, each entry in Providers[]
is registered as an IExternalUserProvider in the Identity child SP.
BindPassword is a SECRET — supply via L5 Override:
Tsak__Contexts__identity.core__Override__Identity__Ldap__Providers__0__BindPassword=<PASSWORD>
Presets: OpenLDAP (UserFilter '(uid={0})') | ActiveDirectory ('(&(objectClass=user)(sAMAccountName={0}))').
Multiple providers tried in Priority order (lower = first). Domains[] routes
user@example.com / EXAMPLE\user to a specific provider.

Providers.//: OpenLDAP example — REMOVE or REPLACE before enabling.
Active Directory (LDAPS) example — REMOVE or REPLACE before enabling. CheckAccountStatus reads userAccountControl bits.

//RecoveryCodePepper: Supply via L5 Override env-var in production. NULL = MFA recovery codes disabled.
Dev ships a fixed value because AllowEphemeralKeys=false is required for the props
signing-key store (see UsePropsSigningKeyStore above), and with ephemeral disabled the
in-code fallback path that generates a random pepper at startup is also disabled.
//SeedBackchannelClient: W6-0 — idempotent client_credentials service account used by the Web BFF
application on startup if missing. ClientSecret is a SECRET — supply via
L5 Override env-var (same value must be set for the Web BFF):
Helper script: redb.Identity/scripts/dev-backchannel-secret.ps1 generates one secret and sets both env-vars.
When ClientSecret is empty the seeder logs a warning and skips (a confidential client cannot be registered without a secret per RFC 6749 §4.4).
//FederationProviders: Each entry is registered as an upstream OIDC IdP (login button on /login).
ClientSecret is a SECRET — supply via L5 Override:
AutoProvision=true creates redb users on first successful login. Priority orders the buttons (lower = first).

FederationProviders.//: Google OIDC — REMOVE or REPLACE before enabling.
Microsoft Entra ID (Azure AD) — multi-tenant. For single-tenant use https://login.microsoftonline.com/<tenant-guid>/v2.0.
Keycloak — replace {host}/{realm}. Self-hosted IdP, useful for testing or B2B federation.
GitHub OAuth2 (NOT full OIDC — userinfo via /user). Authority points at the OIDC bridge endpoint.
Mock OIDC IdP for demo_federation_e2e. Points at navikt/mock-oauth2-server running on the dev rinat-net docker network. Accepts any clientId/clientSecret. REMOVE for production deployments.

Mock GitHub OAuth2 IdP for demo_federation_github. The demo itself starts a tiny pwsh HttpListener on port 9201 that emulates github.com's /login/oauth/authorize + /login/oauth/access_token + api.github.com's /user + /user/emails. REMOVE for production.

//: Secure-by-default: TrustForwardedFor=false ignores X-Forwarded-For.
Enable ONLY when behind a trusted proxy listed below.
KnownProxies  — exact IPv4/IPv6 of trusted proxies, e.g. ['10.0.0.1','203.0.113.7']
C1: per-IP and per-(IP+username) throttling on /connect/token and /login.
Backend 'memory' = per-process (single-node only — counters are NOT shared).
Backend 'redis'  = cluster-wide. REQUIRED behind multiple instances or LB.
Redis examples (supply via L5 Override — connection string is a SECRET):
single  : redis://localhost:6379,abortConnect=false
pwd+db  : redis://:password@redis.internal:6379,defaultDatabase=2,abortConnect=false
cluster : redis-1:6379,redis-2:6379,redis-3:6379,password=<PASSWORD>
sentinel: redis-sent-1:26379,redis-sent-2:26379,serviceName=mymaster
Server-side input-length limits. Regex overrides: leave null for source-generated defaults.
Password length/policy lives in 'PasswordPolicy' below.
H10 — server-side password policy applied by user create/change/SCIM endpoints.
Defaults are STRICT (NIST SP 800-63B / OWASP ASVS 4.0.3 §2.1).
HistoryCount=0 disables history; MaxAge='00:00:00' disables expiration.
BreachCheckEnabled=true requires a registered IPasswordBreachChecker.
N-3 (N3-7) — public self-service account registration.
Disabled by default; enable to bind direct-vm://identity-account-register
and POST /api/v1/identity/account/register.
N-4 (Session C) — SMTP transport for password-recovery / verify-email / change-email.
Dev defaults point at the GreenMail container (route-greenmail) running on the host:
SMTP 3025 (no auth), REST API on 8080 — used by demos to fetch captured mail.
Production hosts MUST replace Host/Port with a real relay and set Security=StartTls or Ssl
with valid credentials. Leave Enabled=false to keep SMTP route + channel unregistered.
C6 — encrypted federation 'state' parameter hardening.
Backend 'memory' is per-process; use 'redis' behind multiple instances.
RedisConnectionString falls back to RateLimit.RedisConnectionString when null
(set ONE Redis target and both subsystems share it). See RateLimit above for
connection-string examples.

//identity.http: Read by the HTTP facade (IdentityHttpModuleHost + IdentityTransportOptions binder).
IdentityTransport:* keys live here. Core (identity.core) ignores this section.

IdentityTransport.//IssuerNote: Mirror of Identity:Issuer (identity.core context). Tsak isolates context property buckets, so the HTTP facade can't read identity.core's Identity:Issuer directly. Drives discovery URLs + cookie Secure flag (https => Secure).

//: PublicPort       — OIDC protocol + discovery (token, userinfo, .well-known/*).
DEFAULT 5002 chosen to avoid the busy 8080 family (Docker friendly).
Must match redb.Identity.Web appsettings.json -> Identity:Authority.
ManagementPort   — admin API (/api/v1/identity/*, /scim/*). Null = share PublicPort.
PROD: separate firewalled port, e.g. 5003.
CorsEnabled      — required for SPA / native PKCE clients calling token from browser.
CorsRegisteredClientOriginsEnabled — auto-allow origins matching registered client URIs.
AdditionalAllowedOrigins — CSV fallback origins. NEVER '*'.
Http.//ManagementPort: PROD: separate firewalled port, e.g. 5003. Null = share PublicPort (DEV only).
//Ssl: Serve the facade over HTTPS natively (Kestrel, via the redb.Route.Http connector).
When true, SslCertPath MUST point to a PFX and Issuer (both identity.core + identity.http)
MUST use the matching https URL so discovery, iss claims and the Secure cookie flag are
emitted correctly. These values are currently set for the LOCAL OpenID conformance suite,
which runs in Docker and reaches the OP at https://host.docker.internal:5002.
For plain non-Docker dev, set Ssl=false and Issuer back to http://127.0.0.1:5002.
SslCertPassword is a SECRET in production — supply via an L5 Override env-var:
Http.//AdditionalAllowedOrigins: CSV without trailing slash. Example: 'https://app.dev.local,https://staging.example.com'. NEVER '*'.

IdentityTransport.//Features: Mirror of Identity:Features (identity.core context). Tsak isolates context property buckets, so the HTTP facade can't read identity.core's Identity:Features directly — keep this list in sync.
IdentityTransport.//FederationProviders: Mirror of Identity:FederationProviders (identity.core context). Tsak isolates context property buckets, so the HTTP facade can't read identity.core's list directly. The /api/v1/identity/federation-providers/public endpoint and the /login page renderer read from this copy. Keep it in sync with the Core list. ClientSecret is unused by the HTTP facade (login redirects only need ClientId + Authority + Scopes); prod deployments may safely set ClientSecret to empty string here.

FederationProviders.//: Mock OIDC IdP for demo_federation_e2e — mirror of identity.core entry.
Mock GitHub OAuth2 IdP for demo_federation_github — mirror of identity.core entry.
